title: Dietary compositions by commodity group
subtitle: Average per capita dietary energy supply by commodity groups, measured in kilocalories per person per day.
caption:
Dietary compositions by commodity group

Average per capita dietary energy supply by commodity groups, measured in kilocalories per person per day.

This stacked area chart tracks the average per capita dietary energy supply in the United States from 1961 to 2023, measured in kilocalories per person per day. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 4,000 kcal, while the horizontal axis spans the years 1961 through 2023. The total height of the stacked areas rises from just under 3,000 kcal in 1961 to nearly 4,000 kcal by 2023. The bottom layer, labeled "Cereals and grains," remains relatively stable throughout the period, hovering around 600 to 700 kcal. Above it, "Pulses" and "Starchy roots" form thin bands that show minimal change. "Fruits and vegetables" and "Dairy and eggs" occupy mid-level bands that expand slightly over time. The "Meat" layer grows noticeably from 1961 to the early 2000s, then plateaus. "Oils and fats" show a steady increase, becoming one of the larger contributors by 2023. The "Sugar" band expands significantly between 1970 and 2000, then contracts slightly after 2005. "Alcoholic beverages" and "Other" form the topmost layers, with "Other" showing modest growth in the final decade. The visual structure reveals that increases in total caloric supply are driven primarily by growth in oils and fats, sugar (until 2005), and meat, while cereal consumption remains largely unchanged.
